comment_text: Logged trail conditions as of this morning: snow-free from TH to summit. The trail is dry and well-packed. Trekking poles are helpful on the descent but not required. One log across the trail at mile 2 — easily stepped over.
label: hikeable

label: hikeable

comment_text: All clear from bottom to top. Saw maybe 20 other hikers which felt manageable. Trailhead has a pit toilet that was clean and stocked. Trail surface is firm and dry. Don't forget sunscreen — it gets hot in the open sections.
label: hikeable

comment_text: Total closure. The county has closed the forest service road due to flooding and rockfall. Even if you could get to the TH the trail is completely under snow. Multiple party turned back when we were there.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: Decent conditions with some caveats. Bottom third of the trail is snow-free. Middle third has some patches and requires careful footing. Top third is covered but packed down from other hikers. Microspikes helpful.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: Wonderful hiking today. Trail opens up at the top with stunning 360 degree views and absolutely no snow in sight. Recommend starting early to beat the afternoon heat. Trail is well-maintained and signed.
label: hikeable

comment_text: Not hikeable right now. Road is impassable due to a rockslide. Even with clear roads, the trail is completely under snow from bottom to top. We could see from a distance — no exposed trail anywhere. Wait for late summer.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: Impassable. We found the TH gate chained shut — no access due to snow on the road. We tried hiking in from 2 miles back but the snow depth made it impossible without snowshoes. Not worth the effort currently.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: The first 3 miles are completely clear and dry. After the switchbacks the trail picks up some snow coverage (maybe a foot in spots). We were glad to have microspikes. Spectacular scenery though — worth it!
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: The trail is in the best shape I've seen in years. Completely dry, well-maintained, no blowdowns. Parking area was busy but we found a spot quickly. The views of the Cascades were crystal clear from the top.
label: hikeable

comment_text: Just got back from a wonderful day out here. Trail is completely clear of snow. Wildflowers are starting to come up near the upper meadows. Bring plenty of water as there are limited reliable sources right now.
label: hikeable

comment_text: Just returned. Trail conditions: excellent. Zero snow. A few muddy sections after last week's rain but nothing requiring special footwear. The wildflowers were absolutely popping near the ridgeline.
label: hikeable

comment_text: Not hikeable in current conditions. The upper trail is an avalanche zone right now — we could see recent avalanche debris on the hillside across the valley. Road is open but please don't attempt this trail.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: The road is open all the way to the TH. First two miles are clear. Snow starts around mile 2 and gets deeper toward the summit (18-24 inches by the top). Postholing without microspikes. Conditions manageable with proper gear.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: The trail is buried and the road is closed. There was a recent mudslide that has blocked the access road. Additionally, significant avalanche debris was visible on the upper slopes. This trail is unsafe right now — wait it out.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: I was nervous about conditions given recent weather but the trail was perfect. Completely clear, great traction even without poles. The logging road to the TH has some potholes but is passable in a regular car.
label: hikeable

comment_text: Completely impassable. The entire route was under 3-4 feet of snow from mile 0.5 onward. No trail visible. We postholed up to our hips. Strong wind and limited visibility at the upper elevations. Turned back for safety.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: Conditions are dangerous. The trail above mile 2 is buried under 6 feet of wind-packed snow. The TH access road had downed trees blocking it — we had to turn around before even starting the hike.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: The road is open all the way to the TH. First two miles are clear. Snow starts around mile 2 and gets deeper toward the summit (18-24 inches by the top). Postholing without microspikes. Conditions manageable with proper gear.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: Just returned. Trail conditions: excellent. Zero snow. A few muddy sections after last week's rain but nothing requiring special footwear. The wildflowers were absolutely popping near the ridgeline.
label: hikeable

comment_text: The lower trail is completely clear. Snow starts appearing around 4,000 feet elevation. A few icy patches on shaded north-facing slopes but nothing serious. Trekking poles and microspikes recommended for the upper section.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: Gorgeous hike today with friends. Dry trail from start to finish. No road closures, parking fee station was staffed. We hiked the full route including the off-trail scramble to the viewpoint. No issues at all.
label: hikeable

comment_text: Trail has some patchy snow starting around mile 2.5 near the ridgeline. Lower portions are clear and dry. Microspikes helpful but not required if you're careful. Beautiful conditions overall with just a hint of winter lingering.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: Hiked this yesterday. Conditions were mixed — lower trail is perfect, but there's a continuous snow band from about the junction to the summit. Maybe 6-8 inches. Microspikes were needed. Road was open.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: Not hikeable right now. Road is impassable due to a rockslide. Even with clear roads, the trail is completely under snow from bottom to top. We could see from a distance — no exposed trail anywhere. Wait for late summer.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: Not hikeable right now. Road is impassable due to a rockslide. Even with clear roads, the trail is completely under snow from bottom to top. We could see from a distance — no exposed trail anywhere. Wait for late summer.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: The road is open all the way to the TH. First two miles are clear. Snow starts around mile 2 and gets deeper toward the summit (18-24 inches by the top). Postholing without microspikes. Conditions manageable with proper gear.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: Road is open but the last 0.5 mile to TH was icy this morning. Trail itself alternates between clear and snowy sections. Above the lake everything was under a thin crust. Bring traction devices.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: Perfect early season hike! No snow anywhere. Road is open with no closures. The high meadows are coming alive with early blooms. We lingered at the lake for lunch and saw osprey hunting. 10/10 recommend.
label: hikeable

comment_text: No issues to report! Trail is dry and clear all the way through. Encountered a few runners which is a bit frustrating on a narrow trail but overall a great experience. No snow, no road closures.
label: hikeable

comment_text: Full snowpack from TH onwards. This trail is NOT currently hikeable without mountaineering gear. The approach was post-hole hell. We gave up at the first major viewpoint and descended safely. Check back in July.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: Trail is passable but not summer conditions. There's 1-2 feet of snow from the upper meadows to the top. Microspikes essential. The lower forest section is completely clear and pleasant. A gorgeous but demanding hike right now.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: Snow begins just past the main viewpoint. Lower sections are clear and pleasant. Some sections of icy trail on the shaded side of the ridge. We managed fine with poles and microspikes. Fewer people than usual.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: Conditions are 'mostly hikeable' with the right gear. Trail is clear from TH to the creek. After the creek there are intermittent snow patches, becoming continuous above 4,500 feet. Go prepared with traction devices.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: DO NOT GO. Fresh snowfall (3+ feet) in the last 48 hours has made this trail completely unnavigable. We couldn't find the trail at all past the parking area. One member of our group postholed to mid-thigh. Turn back.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: The TH road is closed by Forest Service order due to snow. Even on foot the access is sketchy — icy road, downed trees, and active snowmelt. The trail itself is under several feet of snow. Postpone your trip.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: Decent conditions with some caveats. Bottom third of the trail is snow-free. Middle third has some patches and requires careful footing. Top third is covered but packed down from other hikers. Microspikes helpful.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: The road is open all the way to the TH. First two miles are clear. Snow starts around mile 2 and gets deeper toward the summit (18-24 inches by the top). Postholing without microspikes. Conditions manageable with proper gear.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: Road to TH had some ice this morning but nothing that required chains. Trail is clear for the first 2 miles then snow picks up. Microspikes were helpful on the steeper portions. Plan for extra time if hiking in snow.
label: modest_conditions

comment_text: Fantastic hike! Clear skies, no snow or ice on the trail. Trail crews have done a great job maintaining the path. A few muddy patches near the creek crossing but nothing that required any special gear. Views at the top were incredible.
label: hikeable

comment_text: Hiked with my dog off-leash in a few spots — other hikers were friendly and the trail was empty enough to enjoy. Completely snow-free, nice and dry. Would definitely come back next weekend.
label: hikeable

comment_text: The trail is in the best shape I've seen in years. Completely dry, well-maintained, no blowdowns. Parking area was busy but we found a spot quickly. The views of the Cascades were crystal clear from the top.
label: hikeable

comment_text: Not hikeable in current conditions. The upper trail is an avalanche zone right now — we could see recent avalanche debris on the hillside across the valley. Road is open but please don't attempt this trail.
label: not_hikeable

comment_text: Wonderful hiking today. Trail opens up at the top with stunning 360 degree views and absolutely no snow in sight. Recommend starting early to beat the afternoon heat. Trail is well-maintained and signed.

comment_text: The trail is fully accessible and snow-free. The creek crossing at mile 1.5 is easily fordable with dry boots. Wildflowers in the meadow section were incredible. We'll definitely be back.

comment_text: Trail is in great shape today! Dry conditions from the TH all the way to the summit. No snow encountered anywhere on the route. Parking lot was about half full when I arrived around 9am. Highly recommend for a weekend outing.

comment_text: Hiked this weekend. Trail is accessible but expect some snow in the upper half. The meadow section had about 6 inches of coverage which was manageable. Route finding was easy since the trail was still visible. Great day out.

comment_text: Some lingering snow on the upper sections. Most of the lower trail is ice-free. There's one tricky icy traverse near the summit block — caution advised without microspikes. Otherwise perfectly enjoyable.

comment_text: Mixed conditions depending on elevation. Lower forest trail is mud and roots — clear of snow. As you gain elevation you start hitting snow patches. By the time you reach the exposed ridge it was continuous snow. Microspikes a must.
